name: pdb-database
description: Access RCSB PDB for 3D protein/nucleic acid structures. Search by text/sequence/structure, download coordinates (PDB/mmCIF), retrieve metadata, for structural biology and drug discovery.
capabilities: Search PDB by text, protein name, or keywords
Search by sequence similarity using MMseqs2
Search by 3D structure similarity using BioZernike
Download coordinate files in PDB, mmCIF, or BinaryCIF formats
Retrieve metadata including experimental method, resolution, and deposition date
Perform batch operations across multiple structures
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: This is a legitimate scientific skill for accessing the public RCSB Protein Data Bank. All 232 static findings are false positives. The scanner misinterpreted markdown inline code formatting (backticks) as Ruby shell execution, protein amino acid sequences as Windows SAM database, generic substrings as cryptographic algorithms, and public scientific API endpoints as network threats.
valueStatement: Access the RCSB Protein Data Bank to search for and download 3D structures of proteins and nucleic acids. This skill enables structural biology research, drug discovery, and protein engineering by providing programmatic access to over 200,000 experimentally determined and computationally modeled molecular structures.
userTitle: Search Protein Data Bank for molecular structures
limitations: Requires internet connection to access RCSB PDB public API
Rate limits apply for high-volume requests
Downloaded files require external tools like BioPython for analysis

useCases.title: Find drug-target complexes
useCases.description: Search for protein-ligand complexes to understand binding sites and design new therapeutics.

useCases.title: Compare mutant structures
useCases.description: Find homologous structures and analyze sequence-structure relationships for protein design.

useCases.title: Download structures for analysis
useCases.description: Retrieve 3D coordinates for computational analysis, visualization, and structure-based research.

promptTemplates.title: Basic structure search
promptTemplates.prompt: Find the PDB entry for hemoglobin and tell me its resolution and experimental method.

promptTemplates.title: Sequence search
promptTemplates.prompt: Find proteins with sequences similar to: MTEYKLVVVGAGGVGKSALTIQLIQNHFVDEYDPTIEDSYRKQVVID

promptTemplates.title: Filter by quality
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all human protein structures with resolution better than 2.0 Angstroms determined by X-ray crystallography.

promptTemplates.title: Batch download
promptTemplates.prompt: Download the PDB files for entries 4HHB, 1MBN, and 1GZX in mmCIF format.

bestPractices: Use the Search API to get PDB IDs first, then fetch detailed data to minimize API calls.
Implement rate limiting with delays between requests to avoid hitting API limits.
Cache frequently accessed data locally to reduce redundant network requests.
antiPatterns: Making individual API calls in a loop without delays - this will trigger rate limits.
Downloading entire database when only specific entries are needed.
Not checking API response status codes before processing data.

faq.question: What is the difference between PDB and mmCIF formats?
faq.answer: PDB is the legacy text format with column constraints. mmCIF is the modern standard that handles larger structures and more metadata.

faq.question: How do I parse downloaded structure files?
faq.answer: Use BioPython's PDBParser for PDB files or the mmCIF parser for mmCIF format. Both can iterate through atoms and residues.

faq.question: What is a good resolution for crystal structures?
faq.answer: High-resolution structures are below 2.0 Å. Structures between 2.0-3.0 Å are moderate quality. Above 3.0 Å is lower resolution.

faq.question: How do I search by protein sequence?
faq.answer: Use SequenceQuery with your amino acid sequence. Set evalue_cutoff and identity_cutoff to filter results by similarity.

faq.question: What are biological assemblies?
faq.answer: Biological assemblies represent the functional form of a macromolecule, which may differ from the crystallographic asymmetric unit.

faq.question: Can I search for ligand-bound structures?
faq.answer: Yes, use AttributeQuery with rcsb_nonpolymer_entity_instance_container_identifiers.comp_id to find structures containing specific ligands like ATP or heme.
